title: Senior Full Stack Engineer, Battlegrounds Lead
hook: Lead the Hearthstone Deck Tracker and HSReplay.net companion products. A role where being a top Battlegrounds player is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have.

apply.subject: Senior Full Stack Engineer
includes: A short note on what excites you about the role
Your highest Battlegrounds MMR, plus your least and most favorite seasonal mechanic
A link to your portfolio or a project (optional but decisive)

unfairGate.title: The gate most engineers cannot pass: 8000+ MMR
unfairGate.detail: The JD asks you to have hit the Battlegrounds leaderboard (8000+ MMR) and to name your favorite and least favorite seasonal mechanic. Almost no senior C# and WPF engineer can hit 8000 MMR, and almost no 8000-MMR player can ship production C# and WPF. The intersection is tiny, and that intersection is the whole moat. This course meets you where you are: if you already play at a high level you add the engineering, and if you already engineer you add the ladder grind and a Battlegrounds tool. Both on-ramps converge on the same proof.

competencies.name: C# and WPF/XAML desktop
competencies.jdQuote: Strong expertise in C#, with prior professional WPF/XAML experience. Maintain our primary C# desktop software Hearthstone Deck Tracker.
competencies.proof: A working WPF/XAML desktop overlay that reads the Hearthstone log and shows live Battlegrounds info.

competencies.name: TypeScript and React
competencies.jdQuote: Update our accompanying web platform built in Typescript/React.
competencies.proof: A React page that renders Battlegrounds stats pulled from an API.

competencies.name: Python and Django
competencies.jdQuote: and Python/Django. Integrate and enhance back-end web APIs used by our software.
competencies.proof: A small Django API that stores and serves your tool's match data.

competencies.name: Postgres and Redshift SQL
competencies.jdQuote: Prior experience with relational SQL Databases, especially Postgres or Amazon Redshift.
competencies.proof: A normalized Postgres schema plus the queries that power your stats page.

competencies.name: Mixpanel and Grafana dashboards
competencies.jdQuote: Build and maintain sophisticated dashboards using Mixpanel and Grafana.
competencies.proof: A Grafana or Mixpanel dashboard of your own tool's real usage.

competencies.name: Testing, CD, profiling
competencies.jdQuote: Ensure high code quality with automated testing, work within a Continuous Deployment environment, use debugging and profiling tools.
competencies.proof: The tool repo with unit tests and a GitHub Actions pipeline that ships on merge.

competencies.name: Battlegrounds mastery, 8000+ MMR
competencies.jdQuote: You have hit the leaderboard (8000+ MMR) at least once.
competencies.proof: A screenshot of your account at 8000+ MMR. This is a gradeable, undeniable credential.

competencies.name: Community presence
competencies.jdQuote: Regular participation in Battlegrounds communities on Reddit, Twitch and/or Discord.
competencies.proof: An active profile: posts, a small stream, or helpful Discord presence tied to your tool.

capstone.title: Ship an open-source Battlegrounds companion tool
capstone.detail: One project proves every requirement at once: a small open-source Hearthstone Battlegrounds companion. A C#/WPF overlay that reads the game log, a TypeScript/React stats page backed by a Python/Django API and Postgres, instrumented with a Grafana dashboard, tested, and shipped via CI. It is a miniature of exactly what HearthSim builds, so it doubles as your interview.

onRamps.from: You already play at a high level
onRamps.plan: You have the domain and the gate is within reach. Front-load the engineering: learn C# and WPF by building the overlay first, then add the React and Django web layer. Your MMR and community presence are already your differentiator.

You have the stack, so the gap is the game. Grind the ladder toward 8000 while you build the companion tool, and get active in the Battlegrounds Discord and subreddit. The tool plus the MMR screenshot makes your passion undeniable.

applicationKit: Resume that leads with the companion tool you built and your MMR, not a wall of generic experience.
Cover note that answers their exact questions: highest MMR, least and most favorite seasonal mechanic, and one line on the tool you shipped.
Portfolio link to the open-source repo plus a 60 second demo video.
Send to jobs@hearthsim.net with subject 'Senior Full Stack Engineer'.
